candidates.stmt:
I am standing to be your County Councillor because Acle needs strong, competent representation that delivers results, not more talk.

I bring over 30 years’ experience in operational delivery across the private sector and the NHS, focused on improving efficiency, reducing costs, and making services work properly. I am the co-founder of a telecoms software business that exports globally, which I have helped run for the past decade. That experience means I understand how to get things done, control spending, and deliver real value.

Locally, I have served as a Broadland District Councillor, where I chaired the Service Improvement and Efficiency Committee and served as Vice Chair of Licensing. I know how councils operate, where money is wasted, and how to challenge it effectively.

Until recently, I served as a magistrate for seven years, including as a Presiding Justice. I stepped down in 2025 so I could speak more openly about the challenges facing our justice system and the need for practical reform. I also served in the infantry of the Territorial Army, which instilled in me discipline, resilience, and a strong sense of duty.

Alongside this, I founded the first NGO in south-east Botswana focused on improving children’s access to education and healthcare.

If elected, I will:

- Drive efficiency and value for money at County Hall
- Challenge wasteful spending and poor decision-making
- Be a strong, visible voice for local residents
- Hold leadership and officers properly to account

I have lived locally in Blofield for 11 years, and understand the issues that matter to our community.

This election is a clear choice. In Acle, it is Reform or Green. You can vote for more soft talk, or you can vote for someone with a proven track record of delivery, who will stand up, challenge the system, and get things done.

I am standing as an independent candidate on 7th May to SERVE all the residents in the Acle Division of Norfolk County Council – free from political party influence or control.
My actions will be guided by what YOU communicate to me.
I have lived with my family in Broadland for just under 20 years. I am an accountant by profession and have worked for the Audit Commission as well as the Norfolk County Council’s Audit Service.
My public service started with the Brundall Parish Council (2009 to 2020) followed by a move to the Freethorpe Parish Council (2020 to 2025).
I was elected to represent the Marshes Ward of the Broadland District Council in 2015, 2019 and 2023 and continue until it no longer exists.
I am most proud of having established the Parish Council Cluster group in 2013 which now includes Members from Acle, Halvergate, Freethorpe, Reedham, Cantley, Beighton, Lingwood, Strumpshaw, Brundall, Blofield and Postwick Parish Councils. This group meets regularly to share mutual concerns and ideas and collectively lobby higher authorities and institutions for necessary change to benefit their residents.

I’m Taila, and I’m standing in the upcoming County Council election for the Attleborough Division.

I was born and raised in Attleborough, and I’ve been proud to serve our community for over 10 years — currently as Deputy Mayor and as a Breckland District Councillor for the Attleborough Burgh & Haverscroft Ward. Public service runs in my family, and I’m proud to be the third generation to serve our town.

My biggest inspiration is my daughter, Paisley. As a mum to an autistic child, I understand how challenging it can be for families to access the right support, which is why improving SEND services and building a kinder, more inclusive community means so much to me.

I’m a councillor who believes in doing the right thing — not the easy thing. I’ve always stepped up when our town needed it, whether it was organising community support during the pandemic or helping residents directly during the 2023 floods. I care deeply about this place and the people in it, and I’ll always roll up my sleeves when needed.

My priorities:

• Making sure Attleborough gets the infrastructure it needs as we grow

• Improving SEND support for children, young people and adults

• Working to reduce flood risk in Attleborough and Besthorpe

• Ensuring public money is used wisely and effectively

If I’m elected, you’ll have a strong, consistent voice representing you across all three levels of local government — someone who will always listen and always fight for what’s best for our community, championing the needs of Attleborough and Besthorpe every step of the way.

"Sue is keen to be re-elected to Broadland District Council. Currently engaged in part-time voluntary work with a local advice agency, Sue lives with her husband, Richard, in Burgh Road, Aylsham. She has two adult sons and a history of business, science at UEA and HR at Norfolk County Council. Her first experience of self-employment was running a village shop and post office. Things grew from there and, having started and successfully grown a business in machine manufacturing (Samplex, now Tekpro in North Walsham) , Sue more recently had a small tourism business arranging and managing coach tours to France.

Since being elected last May, Sue has joined the Overview and Scrutiny Committee, the Wellbeing Panel and is a representative on the Broadland Group of Children's Centres Partnership Board. Sue says "It is satisfying to be part of a committee, researching, contributing, discussing and making recommendations to the council Cabinet over such things as the new Broadland Business Plan, policies like the collection of Council Tax debt and contracts with suppliers of services".

"Enquiries are received on a regular basis from residents in our ward and I enjoy looking into the issues and providing information or a way forward. For instance, I have recently met with the new Highways Engineer from Norfolk County Council to look at various junction, roads and pavement problems around Aylsham."

"Attending meetings and staying on top of issues raised by local people has been my priority. I try to seek and listen carefully to residents' opinion so that I can truly represent local views".

The fundamental reason for Sue being involved in politics is that she believes that everyone, regardless of background, should have the same opportunities in life, at school, in the workplace and in the community and that this begins with tolerance and understanding.

Broadland District Council currently has 47 Councillors, only 12 of which are women. Currently, only 6 are not Co

For over 30 years of living in the area, Jan has worked tirelessly to support the local community as a parish councillor and volunteer. Jan knows the area well and understands local concerns. As a district councillor and Cabinet member for Environment at Broadland District Council, Jan is keen to use his experience in a county councillor role in local government reorganisation and the transition to new authorities.

Being a cabinet member of the district council has been hard work and a privilege, collaborating with staff and elected members to run an effect council delivering reliable services. Jan says 'Your choice in this election is clear, it's either the Reform party whose track record has failed to deliver, or you can vote for me on 7th May to support vital services and infrastructure improvements across the Blofield & Brundall area.'

Jan will continue his regular contact with Anglian Water to push for improved sewerage systems and clean water with adequate supplies to existing and new homes. We all know our local doctors, dentists, schools, pharmacies, and care systems are all under pressure through years of underfunding by Conservative governments. More housing means more people, and we need to invest to ensure adequate future services.

Green candidate, Jan Davis, said, 'As your councillor, I'll continue to work hard with the experience and contacts I have built up in local government and the private sector to improve services.'

I founded my internet communications business here in Norfolk over 27 years ago and I’ve lived in Blofield for 11 years.  I care passionately about our country and Norfolk, I’ve have been a yachtsman on the Norfolk Broads for over 40 years, I’ve seen the decline and I want to do all I can to fix it.

The Uniparty cancelled last year’s county elections and tried to again this year. Thanks to Reform UK taking the government to court we have our elections on May 7th 2026. I will stand for democracy and make your voices heard.  I will champion local business and services, ensuring the council cuts waste and delivers real value for money, so you get the best possible services for the council tax you pay.

I’ve lived in Bowthorpe for over two decades and feel deeply for the community that I live in. I am standing for election in Bowthorpe as I want to offer a real, hopeful alternative to the traditional two-party system that has compassion and grassroots organisation at its core. I’ve spent my adult life working in health and social care, including at the NNUH, St Martins Housing Trust, New Routes Integration and as a frontline worker during the COVID-19 pandemic.

In 2026, life is hard for so many; expensive bills, inefficient services, inadequate mental health support, benefit cuts for the unwell and disabled, a crumbling NHS and no new social and council housing. If elected, I would like to support those struggling with the cost of living, tackle antisocial behaviour, advocate for residents regarding the future of the ward and protect and promote local green spaces, such as the Bowthorpe Marsh and Bowthorpe Southern Park.

A vote for me is a vote for a local girl who genuinely cares about those around her and who will always call out corruption, collusion and injustice.

I have always liked to give something back to my community whenever I can. Since joining the Liberal Democrats I have been encouraged to engage in local politics, campaigning to improve my local area and the lives of fellow residents.

Voter participation and engaged democracy are things I care for passionately and have worked to improve all of my adult life, particularly amongst younger voters. Whilst campaigning for Remain in the EU referendum, I spoke and took part in events motivating voters to sign up and make their voices heard and I believe that the public should be given the opportunity to have a vote on the final Brexit deal.

I am also passionate about the environment, as a member of several organisations and volunteering with conservation groups.

Born in Norwich, I live locally in Sewell, and work in the city centre.

I have lived within the Great Yarmouth Borough all my adult life, born from a military family and been a public servant in Norfolk for 30 years.

I joined Norfolk Constabulary as a Police Constable in 1990 my initial posting was Great Yarmouth and within those early years moved around the Borough covering Gorleston, the southern Villages of Belton to Fritton and later the northern side of the Borough of Caister to Martham.

I returned some years later as a Sergeant leading a team covering the same areas. My role was focussed on frontline policing and everything that comes along with serving the community. Later years took me working at various departments within Norfolk Headquarters including seconded to working on a Business Support Review identifying efficiency savings throughout all supporting police functions while improving productivity. I was also attached for 25 of these years to the Firearms Response team and Royalty Protection Unit being a Close Protection Officer in the county for the Royal Family and protected politicians.

My focus now is to represent Great Yarmouth First drawing on my
experience to deliver improved services, striving for efficiency and value for money to the residents of Great Yarmouth.

"Right now feels like a time where people are looking for change, and I see that as an opportunity to do things a bit differently at a local level. I’m particularly focused on the everyday things that make a real difference to people’s lives.

I also care about supporting vulnerable people. How we look after those who need support, including older members of our community, says a lot about who we are.

Growing up in the area gave me a strong sense of community. We know we have something good here, and we want to keep it that way. What unites us all is a genuine care for our community, our environment and each other, and that is something I am passionate about building on."

I would like to serve Catton Grove as it’s Councillor (City/County) by:
• Attend meetings to put forward your views and vote for resolutions 
• Take local issues seriously and do my upmost to resolve
• I would look to provide the best services as possible whilst being as efficient as possible
• I will work hard to try and reduce the 900 million pounds debt which currently incurs 200 million pounds interest per year. Just to put that in context it’s around 100, 000 households paying council tax to pay the interests, and when you factor in loan payments it’s probably nearly 50% of Norfolks households paying for the debt, which means around 50% of households are paying for 100% of the services ( This is only an estimate as I do not know what the payments are on the loan balance) - either way it’s an awful situation to be in

Tony has lived in Norwich since 2009 and has been a member of the Green Party for nearly 30 years. He has previously been a Green Party councillor in Dereham, having been elected to Dereham Town Council three times and to Breckland District Council for one four-year term.

Tony ran a wholefood shop in Dereham for 17 years until 2002. He was chairman of Dereham Chamber of Trade for two years. He also co-wrote the proposal that brought the local “Nipper” bus service to Dereham. He now runs an organic veg-box scheme based here in Norwich.

He has chosen to stand to represent the Green Party because he feels impressed by the caring and responsive manner with which it tackles issues facing our community today. He is particularly interested in supporting local businesses and shops to help make our local community more vibrant. He believes that the old saying ‘think global, act local’ is still relevant today and his actions are a testament to that.

He has been pleased to champion a more holistic, sustainable way of life and would be proud to represent the Green Party again on a local council.

When not campaigning, Tony can be found playing table tennis, performing music, or out trying to learn birdsongs on the meadows by the River Wensum.

As a hopeful Cllr in the next election, I will
Fight for our constituents in all causes
Will listen to concerns and act in best way possible
Be transparrent in how we will fix our Council and try to clear the enormous debt the previous council has left
Save tax payers money where possible
Be a voice of reason and change, to other parties in the council

These are just some of the important policies.

WE NEED CHANGE, WE NEED REFORM, VOTE REFORM.

I am Gary Blundell, and I am proud to put myself forward once again to serve the residents of Costessey.

Having lived in our community since 2007, I understand the issues that matter most to local people because I experience them every day alongside you. As an already elected councillor, I have remained consistently active in the community—working week in, week out to support residents, respond to concerns, and help find practical solutions.

I believe in being honest about what local government can and cannot do. I won’t make unrealistic promises about cutting taxes or solving national issues that fall outside the remit of a local councillor. What I will promise is this: I will listen to you, I will be accessible, and I will continue to take action on the issues that directly affect our community.

My approach has always been simple—be present, be responsive, and stand up for residents. That is what I have done, and that is what I will continue to do.

If elected, I will keep working hard to ensure Costessey remains a place where residents feel heard, supported, and represented

Paul is your Labour candidate for Norfolk County Council. He is a local teacher and has a track record of getting out and talking to voters all year round. He works alongside the other talented Labour councillors in Crome to get things done.

Labour is delivering across Norwich. Securing £20 million  of Pride in Place funding for the Heartsease and Pilling Park estates is just one way that Labour is making change happen. Vote for Paul on Thursday 7th May to add an invaluable progressive voice at County Hall.

I am Alison Webb and I’ve been selected to stand in the upcoming County Council election for Dereham North.  I love Dereham. I live in Dereham and very passionate about getting the best for our town, our communities and our residents.

I previously worked for the MoD, starting at RAF Swanton Morley as an Administrative Assistant and over the 27 years I was employed I was promoted to the Senior Civil Service. Most of this time, I was employed on Human Resource issues, including Head of Communication for the Tornado and Harrier aircraft which I feel was the area I most enjoyed as it involves people issues.

When I retired in 2010, I wanted to give all my efforts to the people of Dereham. I decided that I would concentrate on renovating a derelict windmill which belonged to Dereham Town Council.  This was a massive challenge, but I did not realise until I asked what community support there was to open the windmill again. Along with my husband, we created a team of Trustees and over 50 volunteers and within 3 years of much hard work raised the £300,000 to restore and reopen it to the public as a Community Exhibition Centre. Then the challenge came to raise more funds to build toilet facilities as it was so popular. Another £250,000 worth of fund raising resulted in the Windmill Community Coffee Shop which is very popular not just to visitors to the Windmill but our residents as well!

I was elected to Breckland Council Dereham Neatherd Ward over 10 years ago. I was then chosen to be on the Breckland Executive Board, a role that I enjoy greatly as it gives opportunities to improve the life of even more residents in the Breckland area. I also led during Covid the ‘Dereham Cares initiative’, which ensured that our Dereham and Toftwood residents had much needed help during lock down of prescription collection/delivery and basic food essentials. More recently I led on making the Dereham Parkrun possible, bringing Dereham Town Council, Breckland Council and Norfolk County Cou

I’ve lived in Dereham all my life and am proud to be standing as your Green Party candidate to represent everyone across the Dereham North and Scarning district. I grew up here, in a working class family like many of you. I’ve seen the struggles that everyday people face all my life and I’ve also seen the unwavering kindness and quiet community spirit that defines who we are. Representing us is not a responsibility I would take lightly.

Right now, hard working people are struggling to make ends meet, roads and essential public services are falling into disrepair, and politicians seem to care more about playing political games than standing up for the people they were trusted to represent. I firmly believe that the Green Party is the party best positioned to deliver real change. Voting Green at all levels sends a clear message that we will no longer tolerate politicians who protect a wealthy few at the expense of us, our communities and our planet.

Should you entrust me with your vote on 7th May, I will use everything at my disposal to tackle our local issues:

- Protecting road users and their vehicles by ensuring our roads are safe, well maintained and properly designed to reduce congestion.

- Reducing stress and anxiety for our parents and children by ensuring there is sufficient SEND provision in our schools.

- Tackling fly-tipping by making our recycling centres more accessible.
Working to reduce flood risk in rural areas.

- Standing up for our marginalised communities by ensuring Norfolk County Council is an inclusive council that strongly opposes discrimination at all levels.

- Connecting our rural communities by pushing for improved availability of bus services, GPs, social care and other essential services.

And most importantly, I will always listen to your ideas and concerns and make sure your voice is heard at county hall. I believe improving our community is something everyone should have the chance to be involved with.

Thank you for taking the ti

Phillip Duigan continues to work hard for the residents of South Dereham including Toftwood as County Councillor and Dereham Town Councillor.  
Phillip has lived all his life in the Washbridge part of Scanning adjacent to Dereham. He attended Swaffham Convent, Beeston Hall, King's Ely Schools and Norwich City College. Subsequently he obtained an Open University Degree and a Masters Degree in History at  the UEA.  He worked as a civilian at RAF Swanton Morley for much of the time in the Education Centre.

Phillip is on the Scrutiny  Committee and Norfolk Records Committee. He has promoted local highways improvements new signage, bus shelters, improved footpaths, and traffic calming  measures. He has campaigned for improved flood prevention measures for Dereham. He supports the County Councils commitment to excellent Museums and libraries.

Phillip is a member of the Dereham Heritage Trust and Dereham Articulate Speakers, and supports the work of The Royal British Legion.

I’m a small business owner and have proudly called Dereham home for just over 10 years. My husband and I chose to build our life here, and I care deeply about the future of our town.

Alongside running my local photography business, I also volunteer in Dereham and across Norfolk, supporting children and adults with SEND along with their families. I am part of an APPG (All Party Parliamentary Group) focused on safeguarding in faith communities, shaped by my own lived experience of leaving a high-control religion and being put through “conversion therapy”.

I understand the pressures facing the local people and families who feel overlooked and forgotten. Too many in our community have been left behind and I want to get your voices heard.

I’m standing to be a strong voice championing SEND families, ensuring vulnerable people get the support they deserve, and standing firmly with the working people of Dereham.

I will make sure Dereham residents get their concerns both heard and acted on!

Having lived here for 5 years and been a regular contributor to The Diss Express and Park Radio I also had my own record shop in Diss for three years. I was part of a community shop No 8 Market place where I felt the real spirit and voice of the town. Last summer I introduced people from the Market to Adrian Ramsey and following the GE result realised there is genuine hope out there. I do owe some of enthusiasm to speaking with people to the Lib Dem’s. I campaigned on council by-elections and national ones also. My good friend Bobby Dean is now an MP, Bobby and I worked closely way back around Brexit.

I see the enthusiasm but enormous struggle within our community to find a positive narrative. I would love to see more investment and time donations put into filling empty shops in Diss with active projects that help relieve the strain put on single parents and families that will soon be taking on the heavy lifting set by Labour’s budget. I feel that Zack’s words can be echoed down into every single community. I would like to see the basics tackled head on, such as ways of tackling climate change in micro actions and bigger ones such as a 20 MPH limit throughout Diss and Roydon. I would like to see post school clubs for children of all ages that look to educate and encourage different practical learning skills. I would love to see local businesses brought in to help donate to these causes but willingly and proudly. An example would be the now empty Aldi which presumably will be empty for months or years to come. I would also like to try and turn heads towards a need for community run entertainment hubs like a cinema.

What excites me is the prospect of people rejecting hate speech from Reform and looking towards what they can do within their community to both help themselves and their town or village. I think once people see Green action within their community they’ll be far more likely to then take on simple acts that help the environment. I would love to be part of

Colin has lived in the Diss area for the last 14 years. He was a police officer, leading a murder squad in London for the last nine of his 30 years’ service.

Since retiring from the police he has written three books, collaborated in writing two ITV dramas and written and presented four seasons of a true crime show for Sky.
Colin is an active member of Diss Golf Club and spends the rest of his spare time caring for and showing his four Curly-Coated Retrievers.

He is the chair of Reform UK Waveney Valley branch, and the national Police and Crime Adviser for Reform UK HQ.

Colin sees being a County Councillor as an extension of his police career. Having spent 30 years trying to protect and serve the community, representing Diss and Roydon would enable him again to make a positive difference in the area where he lives.

Naturally, he has a strong interest in crime and anti-social behaviour, believing that policing should pay particular attention to the quality of life for the law-abiding in the community. He will press for greater visibility of officers throughout the district, foot patrols in the town and for rigorous action to be taken against those who break the law.

He is though also ready to be a champion for other issues in the district – specifically the scourge of the pylons, the disquiet at the proliferation of solar farms and, of course, the dire state of traffic management in the town centre.

Colin’s long career in public service left him angry at the waste of public money, both in how much is paid for supplies and in the awarding of expensive contracts. He will delight in holding the County Council to account for how it spends your council tax.
He believes that the UK, both nationally and locally, is on the brink of becoming broken beyond repair – and that the only chance to repair the damage inflicted by the Tories and now Labour is for Reform UK to take charge.

The Tory County Council conspired with the Labour government to try to cancel your right to

After an art education I moved to Lincoln with my family and ran a vintage clothes shop, long before it was fashionable. With friends I set up Lincoln Live Arts and my shop became a hub of the arts community, we went on to begin the Lincoln Arts Festival.

I have always worked for my community and have constantly organised events. In 1985 I moved with my family to Norwich becoming the Director of Norwich Arts Centre.  Once again, I used my organisational and management skills to build a fantastic artistic community.  I am proud of the many young people we helped through volunteering and part time work into full time paid work.

Bringing up three children I understand the demands and costs of family life and now a grandmother of seven I am aware of today’s pressures and dangers of technology but hope we can embrace these and give our children opportunities and a life to look forward to.  This is why I work so hard for the Labour Party and ask you to elect me on the 7th May.

I moved to Dickleburgh in 2010 and visit Diss at least once a week, often more frequently. We are lucky to have community venues such as the Corm Hall and Market Place, plus a rail link direct to London and Norwich. I have many friends that live in Diss & Roydon and know the area well.

I believe in a community that works together for everyone. I am already leading a campaign to improve access at Diss Rail Station, arguing that we need platform lifts.  I want to help improve local facilities, offering more opportunities for young people. Make sure our elderly are cared for. Make sure we can access doctors when we need them and have good dentistry services.

I will work with organisations and partners to make Diss & Roydon a good and happy place to live.

candidates.n: Michael John Baylis Chenery of Horsbrugh

candidates.stmt:
I will be standing as the Labour candidate for the Downham division of Norfolk County Council on the 7th May.

This is an election for a soon to be abolished council, but important decisions will be made about the future of local government for decades. After three years of representing the place I live at Borough, I am ready to help shape that future, ensuring great services are in place for residents.

Terry Jermy MP and the Labour Government has been delivering for Downham, as we saw with the confirmation of the SEND school at the Ryston End site. We need Norfolk County Council fully focused on delivering the best possible services for residents.

The Tories lost Downham Old Town to me in 2023.
The Tories lost SW Norfolk to Labour in 2024.
The Tories will likely be losing County in 2026.

The Reform Party offer the Tories' failed policies repackaged with a lot more divisive rhetoric that I do not think Norfolk will benefit from. Focusing on who we are against should not be our priority, but instead working together to build a better Norfolk and a fairer society.

Too many live in hardship or poverty, every visit to a shop stressful, and events half a world away will continue to impact on our cost of living. We and our area's precious wildlife are also threatened by the rapidly changing climate, and we must consider and adapt to swiftly evolving technologies.

Yet there is a lot to hope for in Downham Market, with our thriving high street, independent businesses, and many successful charities and organisations tackling some of those issues directly.

Together, we achieve more than we do alone, and that is why I am standing for election on the 7th May.

I am concerned at the scale of the climate and ecological crisis that we are facing, and the wide-spread corruption and failure of our public institutions in performing their basic duties - and the almost complete lack of understanding of these issues among all the political parties, including sadly, the Green Party of which I used to be a member. I was elected me as a district councillor in May 2023 and in this role, among other things, I have successfully stoped a mega-poultry and pig farm from coming to West Norfolk, fought for and obtained guarantees of a competition-grade swimming pool to replace the existing outdated pool, helped several residents obtain warm home heating grants and got a garden wildlife competition underway to encourage people to look after the wildlife in their backyards. I'm a GP by profession, and this involves regularly talking to hundreds of people from all walks of life. I believe this breadth of experience has stood me in good stead in the rough and tumble world of politics and media coverage. I write a monthly Politics opinion column for the Lynn News which has been generally well received. My contributions as vice-chair of the Norfolk health overview scrutiny committee have been praised by journalists as well as colleagues from other parties. 
If elected I will be a champion for Downham Market which has great potential to become a beacon for a sustainable, healthy, and joyful way of life.

Having lived in East Anglia for 30 years, Norfolk is truly home.

I have two daughters in their 20s, so I understand the challenges facing young people and families.

35 years of business experience means I know how to balance priorities and spend money wisely.

As a volunteer adviser with Citizens Advice, I've helped many people through tough times.

I care deeply about this area and will be a visible, responsive councillor who listens and delivers.

"Glenn is one of our two candidades for the Blofield with South Walsham Ward of Broadland District Council. Claire Lees is our other candidate.

Glenn writes “Since coming to Norfolk to attend the UEA in 1998 I have lived much of my time in the Broadland area. My work as a Children’s Advocate means that I see a lot of social injustice – I believe Broadland can be better. I think it is a matter of shame that Broadland District Council sold off all their housing stock in 1990 meaning that families struggle to find good, safe and secure homes. If elected I intend to make this one of my prime concerns as well as fighting for a fairer society for all”"

I am your independent candidate for the upcoming County Council election on Thursday, 7th  May 2026, and with your support, I would like to represent the market town of Harleston & the surrounding villages in the Waveney Valley which form the East Depwade Division on Norfolk County Council.
Local people need someone who will stand-up for them on the local council. I would like to represent you and I hope that you will vote for me on Thursday 7th May 2026.  For those of you that know of me or who have followed my work as a Councillor, it is well known when I make a promise, I keep it. I am never silenced; I am outspoken to stand up for local issues, openness, and transparency in local government. No party or political influence will silence me.
My election material will be dropping through letterboxes across the East Depwade Division which includes the market town of Harleston & the parishes of Billingford, Brockdish, Dickleburgh, Needham, The Pulhams, Rushall, Scole, Starston & Thorpe Abbotts in the coming days and weeks.  I am seeking to be elected on my track record of being a strong local voice for the past 11yrs.  Again feel free to stop me for a chat or reach out and call or email me and I intend to be out with the community speaking to as many residents as possible.
If you want to hear what I have to say locally, follow me on Social Media @claytonhudson drop me an email at mail@claytonhudson.co.uk or call me on landline 01379 430062 or mobile 07868 782560 or goto my website https://claytonhudson.co.uk/

Paul is a Chartered Accountant and has lived and worked in Norfolk since 2000.
Married for 45 years with two daughters who both work and live in Norfolk.
A Reform UK member since 2024, his first political involvement, Paul has a keen interest in healthy and active aging.
He founded and is the Chief Executive of the award-winning Walking Football Association (the WFA), the National Governing body in England for the sport that has transformed, prolonged and saved many lives, improving the mental and physical health of its senior participants, including people living with Parkinsons, Cancer, deafness, and other impairments. Paul has led the development of the England Walking Football teams which won the Sport’s World Cup last year. 
If elected, Paul will promote and encourage a greater focus on healthy lifestyles for all, supporting strong family and British values. Promoting regional economic growth is a key element in our strategy.
Paul has always taken a keen interest in community affairs and is the founder and organiser of his successful village Community Speed Watch team as well as starting the local Walking Football Club in this ward.
The Elmham and Mattishall region, and Norfolk as a county, have enormous potential for business, social and tourism development, and Paul’s extensive business, life experience and skill set will be an asset to the Council’s work and the many social and infrastructure challenges (including the ubiquitous potholes) the area needs to address.
Paul enjoys gardening, travel, sport, chocolate, magic and is a quiz show aficionado, having appeared on 5 National TV Quiz shows.

Bill is the current County Councillor for the Elmham and Mattishall Division and he is delighted to be re-standing in the Elections next May. Bill lives locally in the Division that he represente. Bill believe that it is his job to make sure that the County Council listens to our issues and takes account of them in it's decision making. Bill is very concerned about the Government's massively increased housing targets for our area and its' decision to encourage large Solar Installations (he can't call them farms!). He has also been fighting to improve rural transport and reduce the speed of traffic in our villages.

Age 60 retired solicitor previously working in Kings Lynn, Downham Market, Norwich and self-employed from home.

Married no kids. Lived in Norfolk since 1994 in Kings Lynn, Great Bircham, Dereham, Sparham. Keen dinghy sailor on the north Norfolk coast. Liberal Democrat since 2017.

For those that don’t know me, my name is Rob Jamieson and I have the honour to be standing as the Reform UK candidate in the upcoming Norfolk County Council elections, specifically the Fakenham & Raynhams Division. My two children were born and attend schools here. As a Senior Project Manager, my professional life is focused on delivering multimillion pound flood defence refurbishment projects in Norfolk.  As I care deeply about my local community I also dedicate some of my free time as an elected Town Councillor in Dereham where I am currently fighting to keep the town’s car parks free to use, and to curtail antisocial behaviour as part of a new multidisciplinary task force.  I also volunteer as a local Beaver Scout Leader.
 
If elected to the County Council I will apply my varied and versatile project management skills at this strategic level of local governance and push for efficient service delivery, responsible spending, transparency, and effective communication with residents about how your money is being spent. Were you aware that Norfolk County Council is £884 million in debt, and that £627,000 of our council taxes is being spent on debt interest payments every week?
 
For too long, the main political parties have taken your vote for granted. Reform UK offers something different — straightforward, common-sense politics built on the values of family, community and country. Across the UK, more and more people are supporting Reform UK because they want change, and a party that will put hard working people first.  We are now the biggest political party in the UK

I am not a politician but decided to stand at this election for the Heritage Party to give the people of South West Norfolk a genuine alternative. The buzzword at this election is “change”. Everyone agrees that we need it, but what’s on offer is either more of the same by a different name, change that doesn’t go nearly far enough, or change that takes us further in the wrong direction. 

We have been led down a path of managed decline by successive governments. The current government has spent 14 years ruining our society, culture and economy. 

We are a great nation and we deserve better! We have a glorious history and a wonderful heritage which has been given to us by our forefathers, which we should enjoy and build upon to pass on to our children and grandchildren.

If we are to thrive once more then the 25 year long catastrophic experiment with mass immigration must end. The sexualisation of children, largely through the political LGBTQ++ agenda, must end. Sacrificing the prosperity and freedom of ordinary people on the altar of climate alarmism must end. Warmongering in foreign countries must end.

Instead we must pursue the common sense, socially conservative, family values policies found in the Heritage Party manifesto, that will return our country to the thriving, stable, secure and confident nation that it once was.

Hello everyone,

I am standing for Gaywood North & Central because I believe our community would benefit from a strong, practical and attentive voice.

I am not a career politician. I am a lawyer, educator and criminologist, a mother of four, and above all, a proud resident who understands the everyday realities families face.

Like many parents, I think about my children’s future every day. About good schools, the chance to follow their passions, building a career, finding stable work, affording a home, and one day starting families of their own.

Those hopes do not happen by chance. They are built through a shared effort between families, teachers, community leaders and local authorities.

My priorities reflect that understanding:

- Supporting safer streets and neighbourhoods, particularly for women and girls
- Standing up against bullying and anti-social behaviour
- Creating real opportunities for young people and young adults
- Supporting families and strengthening our community
- Improving health services, including the future of the Queen Elizabeth Hospital
- Ensuring dignity, mobility and proper support for our elderly residents
- Promoting access to justice, especially for those who feel unheard or vulnerable

Not all of these challenges are visible, but they shape people’s lives every single day.

They require more than promises. They require careful listening, empathy, practical thinking, and someone willing to dedicate the time and effort this role truly demands.

As a working mother, a committed community volunteer and a charity fundraiser, I have always tried to contribute where I can.

As your County Councillor, I would be able to do far more.

If elected, I will represent every resident of Gaywood North & Central with integrity, focus and a genuine commitment to making a meaningful difference.

That experience matters - because Norfolk County Council's record demands scrutiny. Council tax has risen by nearly 5% for three consecutive years, yet services are being cut.

The council spent £41.9 million on taxis for children with special needs - more than half its entire roads budget - because it failed for a decade to build enough local SEN school places.

It wrote off £36 million on an abandoned road scheme after ignoring four years of warnings.

Controversially, it’s currently selling off Norse Care just to pay off Norse’s £28m debt it owes to NCC. This will leave Norfolk at the mercy of private care homes.

Norfolk County Council is £900+ MILLION in DEBT and pays £627,000 in interest every week. That’s appalling and poor fiscal managment.

Vote for NORFOLK

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt: Sean is a local writer and communications specialist who has lived in Norwich for almost a decade. Based in Bowthorpe since coming to Norwich to attend the UEA, he has been a proud and active member of our fine city’s community from the very start. He is thrilled to be standing to represent the people of South Norwich as their MP and looks forward to speaking to many of them over the course of the campaign.

Fairer Support for Farmers: Moving away from industrial subsidies toward a system that rewards our local farmers for protecting the soil, restoring hedgerows, and ensuring food security.

Public Transport That Works: We cannot tackle the climate crisis if our villages are stranded. I will fight for a "Rural Transport Guarantee"—investing in frequent, affordable bus services and safer cycling routes to keep us connected.

Warm, Affordable Homes: We need social housing that local people can actually afford to live in, built to the highest energy-efficiency standards to keep your bills low and our landscape protected.

Unity Over Division

National politics currently feels precarious. We are seeing a rise in "Reform" rhetoric that seeks to talk down our progress and divide our neighbors. I believe the people of Nar and Wissey are stronger when we work together.

While some parties focus on what divides us, the Green Party focuses on what connects us: the air we breathe, the water in our rivers, and the strength of our local schools and surgeries. We must not let national volatility distract us from the vital work of protecting our local services.

Hello, my name is Marie and I am standing in this election because I care deeply about this community’s future. I live in Great Ellingham and  I understand the challenges we face, from access to services and transport to wellbeing, and the pressures on farming, the environment and the local economy. I believe looking after our countryside and supporting the rural economy go hand in hand, creating vibrant and sustainable rural communities.

I am passionate about community, nature regeneration, enterprise, creativity and heritage. I will bring a practical, forward-looking approach to the challenges we face. If elected, I will focus on improvements that make everyday life better. This includes rural transport, support for local businesses and young people, and protecting our environment and wildlife while helping to build strong, connected communities.

My professional background is in higher education, including work at Norwich University of the Arts. I have also worked internationally across the arts and creative industries and advised the British Council’s Arts and Creative Economy team. Earlier in my career, my husband and I ran an award-winning shoemaking business in rural Ireland, so I understand the realities of running a small rural enterprise.

I have  spent many years involved in wildlife care and environmental initiatives, including reducing our reliance on plastics.

Even where we may not always agree, I believe in respectful dialogue and practical solutions. If elected, I will listen carefully, understand local concerns, and speak up for Yare and Necton with honesty, fairness and integrity.
